---
title: "Portfolio"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

:::{.section-intro}
A selection of professional, applied, and academic work in urban and environmental planning, GIS, and sustainability science.
:::

:::{.project-grid}

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
ESS 440 Capstone · Spring 2026
:::
### NASA GLOBE Observer — CitSci Migration
:::{.card-tags}
[Citizen Science]{.tag} [Data Systems]{.tag} [Urban Ecology]{.tag} [Fort Collins]{.tag}
:::
Piloting the migration of NASA GLOBE Observer's Mosquito Habitat Mapper to the CitSci platform to improve data redundancy and expand community reporting capacity in Fort Collins. The final report documents project outcomes, platform assessment, stakeholder engagement, and ongoing implementation challenges.

:::{.card-links}
Coming Soon!
:::
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
Professional · Sring 2026
:::
### CSU Micromobility TDM Impact Report
:::{.card-tags}
[Transportation Demand Management]{.tag} [Micromobility]{.tag} [Policy]{.tag}
:::
Authored the 2025 Transportation Demand Management Impact Report entry for Colorado State University's micromobility program on behalf of ATFAB and CSU Transportation, published by the Association for Commuter Transportation. The report documents ridership data, program outcomes, and policy recommendations for campus active transportation infrastructure.

:::{.card-links}
[Full Report (PDF)](media/act_2025tdm_report.pdf){target="_blank"}
[CSU Feature (p. 22)](media/act_2025tdm_report.pdf#page=22){target="_blank"}
:::
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
NR 426 · Spring 2026
:::
### Beaver Conflict & Restoration Analysis Tool
:::{.card-tags}
[ArcGIS Pro]{.tag} [Python]{.tag} [GIS]{.tag} [Spatial Analysis]{.tag} [Hydrology]{.tag}
:::
A spatial screening tool built in Python for ArcGIS Pro that intersects BRAT stream network capacity data with NLCD land cover to score stream segments by human-beaver conflict risk and restoration opportunity. Designed to support planning decisions at the county, watershed, or custom AOI scale using ESRI Living Atlas services as default inputs.

:::{.card-links}
[GitHub](https://github.com/zcramton/csu_nr_426/tree/main/FinalProject){target="_blank"}
:::
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
Professional · Spring 2026
:::
### The Spoke — Knowledge Database
:::{.card-tags}
[Knowledge Management]{.tag} [Operations]{.tag} [Bike Shop]{.tag}
:::
Designed and built an internal knowledge database for The Spoke, CSU's on-campus bike cooperative, consolidating product documentation, service history frameworks, parts sourcing references, and operational procedures into a structured, searchable system for volunteer mechanics and staff.
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
Professional · 2024–2025
:::
### ATFAB Web Redesign & Records System
:::{.card-tags}
[Web Design]{.tag} [Knowledge Management]{.tag} [Transportation]{.tag}
:::
Led a comprehensive web redesign and records overhaul for CSU's Alternative Transportation Fee Advisory Board. Rebuilt the public-facing site for clarity and accessibility, and developed an internal knowledge database to standardize institutional records, meeting documentation, and policy tracking across board transitions.
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
ESS 555 · Spring 2025
:::
### Life Cycle Assessment — Soap Refill Packaging
:::{.card-tags}
[LCA]{.tag} [Sustainability Science]{.tag} [OpenLCA]{.tag} [ecoinvent]{.tag}
:::
A comparative life cycle inventory and impact assessment of plastic bottle versus laminate pouch soap refill containers, using OpenLCA with the ecoinvent 3.11 database and ReCiPe 2016 midpoint methodology. Monte Carlo uncertainty analysis was used to evaluate result robustness. Findings suggest refill pouches carry equal or greater environmental impact than bottles across most impact categories — contrary to common marketing claims.

:::{.card-links}
[LCI Report (PDF)](media/ess555_lci_report.pdf){target="_blank"}
[LCIA Report (PDF)](media/ess555_final_report.pdf){target="_blank"}
:::
:::

:::{.project-card}
:::{.card-meta}
ESS 312 · Spring 2025
:::
### Starship Delivery Robots — Campus Perception Study
:::{.card-tags}
[Social Science]{.tag} [Mixed Methods]{.tag} [Transportation]{.tag} [Sustainability]{.tag}
:::
A mixed-methods study assessing student perceptions of Starship food delivery robots at CSU using in-person interviews, Likert-scale surveys, and thematic analysis. Findings revealed predominantly negative sentiment centered on inefficiency, resource misprioritization, and concerns that the robots displace more sustainable alternatives like walking and cycling.

:::{.card-links}
[Report (PDF)](media/ess312_final_report.pdf){target="_blank"}
:::
:::

:::

## Photography

:::{.section-intro}
I'm an amateur photographer with an interest in macro, landscape, nature, and aerial drone photography along the Colorado Front Range. A dedicated gallery is in development.

Remote Pilot Certificate (FAA Part 107): Issued Jan 2026.
:::
